mission.title: openeverest: Installation/namespace provisioning stuck with ImagePullBackOff for the bitnami/kubectl image
mission.description: Installation or namespace provisioning is stuck in the 'Provisioning database namespace' step because the everest-operators-installer job cannot pull the bitnami/kubectl image due to Docker Hub rate limits or network restrictions.

steps.title: Identify the stuck installation
steps.description:
Check for stuck pods in the everest-system namespace:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n everest-system
kubectl get pods -l job-name=everest-operators-installer --all-namespaces
```
Look for pods in ImagePullBackOff or ErrImagePull state.

steps.title: Diagnose the image pull failure
steps.description:
Describe the stuck pod to see the exact image pull error:
```bash
kubectl describe pod -l job-name=everest-operators-installer -n everest-system
```
Look for events showing rate limiting (429 Too Many Requests) or network timeout errors on the bitnami/kubectl image.

steps.title: Apply the fix
steps.description:
**Option A: Create an image pull secret for Docker Hub**
```bash
kubectl create secret docker-registry dockerhub-creds \
  --docker-server=https://index.docker.io/v1/ \
  --docker-username=<your-username> \
  --docker-password=<your-token> \
  -n everest-system
```

**Option B: Mirror the bitnami/kubectl image to your private registry**
```bash
docker pull bitnami/kubectl:latest
docker tag bitnami/kubectl:latest <your-registry>/bitnami/kubectl:latest
docker push <your-registry>/bitnami/kubectl:latest
```
Then update the job to use your mirrored image.

**Option C: Delete the stuck job and retry**
```bash
kubectl delete job everest-operators-installer -n everest-system
```
Then re-run the installation.

steps.title: Confirm the issue is resolved
steps.description:
Verify that the installation proceeds and pods reach Running state:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n everest-system
kubectl get jobs -n everest-system
```
Confirm that all pods are Running or Completed.

resolution.summary: The installation hangs because the everest-operators-installer job fails to pull the bitnami/kubectl image from Docker Hub, typically due to rate limits. The fix is to provide Docker Hub credentials via an image pull secret, mirror the image to a private registry, or retry when rate limits reset.

prerequisites.description: A Kubernetes cluster where OpenEverest installation is stuck at namespace provisioning.
